title: Paronychia pontica
taxonomy: Paronychia pontica is classified in the family Nyctaginaceae and belongs to the genus Paronychia. It is an annual or biennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The genus Paronychia is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as 'milk purslane' or 'pus anemone'. Paronychia pontica is a specific species within this genus. It is characterized by its small white or pink flowers and narrow leaves.
